strengths: Deep TypeScript/Node.js production experience across multiple senior roles, directly matching the primary stack
Active AI agent and MCP infrastructure work at current role (re:cinq), satisfying the AI agent development requirement explicitly
Strong testing culture demonstrated through TDD practice, open-source test tooling authorship, and workshop facilitation — rare at this level
concerns: No Nest.js experience mentioned anywhere in the resume, leaving a gap in the primary framework
React exposure is limited and secondary to Ember.js throughout most of career history, suggesting frontend depth may not match backend depth
Kotlin/Java experience is dated and confined to non-product contexts (robotics tooling at Altom, 2016–2018), not recent backend service work
response.justification: The candidate brings 15+ years of polyglot full-stack experience with a strong current-role match in TypeScript, Node.js, AWS, and MongoDB, and stands out for hands-on AI agent infrastructure work that few candidates at this level can claim. The primary risk is the absence of Nest.js anywhere in the resume and a frontend history dominated by Ember.js rather than React, which could require meaningful ramp-up on the primary framework and modern frontend patterns; Kotlin/Java familiarity exists but is too dated to treat as a working skill for the secondary backend services.
